triage_stuck_ros.name: triage_stuck_ros
triage_stuck_ros.description: Find all stuck and failed ROs, rank by time stuck and Red health flags, recommend escalation priority

steps.description: Find all stuck ROs
steps.sql: SELECT *, DATEDIFF('day', CAST(FILE_RECEIVED_DT AS TIMESTAMP), CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) as days_stuck FROM roster WHERE IS_STUCK = 1 ORDER BY days_stuck DESC

steps.description: Count Red health flags per RO
Assign priority based on days stuck and Red flags
For each stuck RO's state, fetch market SCS_PERCENT from metrics table
steps.sql: SELECT MARKET, SCS_PERCENT, MONTH FROM metrics WHERE MARKET IN ({states}) ORDER BY MONTH DESC

steps.description: Generate stuck RO tracker visualization

record_quality_audit.name: record_quality_audit
record_quality_audit.description: For a given state or org, compute failure rates, health flag distribution, and flag files below quality threshold

steps.description: Get failure stats by state/org
steps.sql: SELECT CNT_STATE, ORG_NM, COUNT(*) as total_files, SUM(CASE WHEN IS_FAILED = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as failed_files, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN IS_FAILED = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) as failure_rate FROM roster WHERE {filter} GROUP BY CNT_STATE, ORG_NM ORDER BY failure_rate DESC

steps.description: Compute Red health flag counts per org
Categorize by FAILURE_STATUS
steps.sql: SELECT FAILURE_STATUS, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM roster WHERE IS_FAILED = 1 AND {filter} GROUP BY FAILURE_STATUS ORDER BY cnt DESC

steps.description: Flag orgs with failure rate above threshold
Stacked bar chart of failure types per state/org

changes.summary: Include SKIP_REC_CNT in the quality calculation.
Include SKIP_REC_CNT in the quality calculation.
Include SKIP_REC_CNT in the quality calculation

market_health_report.name: market_health_report
market_health_report.description: Correlate a market's SCS_PERCENT (CSV2) with pipeline-level file failure rates (CSV1) for the same state

steps.description: Get market SCS_PERCENT trend
steps.sql: SELECT MONTH, MARKET, SCS_PERCENT, FIRST_ITER_SCS_CNT, FIRST_ITER_FAIL_CNT, NEXT_ITER_SCS_CNT, OVERALL_SCS_CNT, OVERALL_FAIL_CNT FROM metrics WHERE MARKET = '{market}' ORDER BY MONTH

steps.description: Get file-level metrics for same state
steps.sql: SELECT STRFTIME(CAST(FILE_RECEIVED_DT AS TIMESTAMP), '%m-%Y') as month, COUNT(*) as total_files, SUM(CASE WHEN IS_FAILED = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as failed_files, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN IS_FAILED = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) as file_failure_rate FROM roster WHERE CNT_STATE = '{state}' GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

steps.description: Compare market SCS_PERCENT with file failure rates to identify correlated declines
Identify top failing orgs in this state from CSV1
steps.sql: SELECT ORG_NM, COUNT(*) as files, SUM(CASE WHEN IS_FAILED = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as failures FROM roster WHERE CNT_STATE = '{state}' GROUP BY ORG_NM ORDER BY failures DESC LIMIT 10

steps.description: Dual-axis chart: SCS_PERCENT trend + file failure rate

retry_effectiveness_analysis.name: retry_effectiveness_analysis
retry_effectiveness_analysis.description: Compare first-pass vs retry success rates to determine if reprocessing improves outcomes

steps.description: Find ROs with retries (RUN_NO > 1)
steps.sql: SELECT RO_ID, RUN_NO, LATEST_STAGE_NM, IS_FAILED, IS_STUCK, FAILURE_STATUS, FILE_RECEIVED_DT FROM roster WHERE RO_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT RO_ID FROM roster WHERE RUN_NO > 1) ORDER BY RO_ID, RUN_NO

steps.description: Compare first run vs latest retry outcome per RO
steps.logic: For each RO: compare RUN_NO=1 (IS_FAILED, LATEST_STAGE_NM) vs max(RUN_NO) result

steps.description: Market-level retry lift from CSV2
steps.sql: SELECT MARKET, MONTH, FIRST_ITER_SCS_CNT, NEXT_ITER_SCS_CNT, OVERALL_SCS_CNT, ROUND((NEXT_ITER_SCS_CNT - FIRST_ITER_SCS_CNT) * 100.0 / NULLIF(FIRST_ITER_SCS_CNT, 0), 2) as retry_lift_pct FROM metrics ORDER BY MARKET, MONTH

steps.description: Identify ROs that never improved despite retries
steps.logic: ROs where max(RUN_NO) > 1 AND still IS_FAILED=1

steps.description: Grouped bar chart: first-iter vs overall success by market
